plan_metrics.plan_content:
Here's a summary of the full plan before you approve:

---

## Plan: Add `ImageTextToTextPipeline` to `src/pipelines.js`

**Only one file changes — `src/pipelines.js` — with 4 targeted edits.**

### What I found during exploration

- `AutoModelForImageTextToText` already exists in `src/models.js` (line 8736), supporting Florence-2, LLaVA, SmolVLM, Qwen2-VL and 7 other architectures — no model-side work needed.
- Its internal forward pass (`imageTextToTextForward`, models.js:819) expects `pixel_values` for the image and `input_ids` for text — both go into `model.generate()` together.
- There is no pipeline class for it yet — this is exactly what the task requires.

### The 4 edits

| # | Location | What |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `import` block (~line 44) | Add `AutoModelForImageTextToText` to the `./models.js` import |
| 2 | After `ImageToTextPipeline` (~line 2005) | Insert the JSDoc typedefs + `export class ImageTextToTextPipeline` |
| 3 | `SUPPORTED_TASKS` (~line 3255) | Add `"image-text-to-text"` entry after `"image-to-text"` |
| 4 | `pipeline()` JSDoc (~line 3427) | Add the `"image-text-to-text"` bullet between `"image-to-text"` and `"object-detection"` |

### `_call` design
```js
async _call(image, text, generate_kwargs = {}) {
    const { pixel_values } = await this.processor((await prepareImages(image))[0]);
    const { input_ids } = this.tokenizer(text, { add_special_tokens: false, padding: true, truncation: true });
    const output = await this.model.generate({ inputs: input_ids, pixel_values, ...generate_kwargs });
    return this.tokenizer.batch_decode(output, { skip_special_tokens: true })
               .map(x => ({ generated_text: x.trim() }));
}
```
- Two-arg signature `(image, text)` — mirrors `DocumentQuestionAnsweringPipeline`
- Passes **both** `input_ids` and `pixel_values` to generate — required by the multimodal forward pass
- Returns `[{ generated_text: string }]` as specified
- Default model: `"onnx-community/Florence-2-base-ft"`

details.command: node -e "const p = require('./src/pipelines.js'); if (typeof p.ImageTextToTextPipeline !== 'function') { process.exit(1); } console.log('OK')"
